summary.name: Cross-Asset Momentum Rotation Vol-Scaled
summary.author: Pardhu Nani
summary.description: The strategy is a cross asset trend and relative momentum rotation that seeks to capture persistent medium term price trends across major ETFs while controlling risk through volatility scaling. summary.description_full:
The strategy is a cross asset trend and relative momentum rotation that seeks to capture persistent medium term price trends across major ETFs while controlling risk through volatility scaling. It evaluates a predefined universe spanning US and international equities, real estate, gold, investment grade credit, and US Treasuries and broad bonds. The signal is a composite momentum score built from multiple lookback horizons approximating 1, 3, 6, and 12 months of daily data, designed to favor assets with consistent positive trends across time scales and to shift exposure as leadership changes between risk on and defensive regimes.

Portfolio decisions are made monthly at the start of each month using end of day price history (with a long warmup to ensure indicators are stable). The strategy ranks all eligible ETFs by the composite momentum score and holds the top five. Position sizing is inverse volatility weighted using the standard deviation of recent daily returns over roughly three months, so more volatile assets receive smaller weights and total risk is balanced across holdings. At each rebalance it exits unselected ETFs and reweights the selected set to the new targets, remaining fully invested among the selected assets. The intended alpha source is time series and cross sectional momentum combined with risk parity style scaling.
